bullets.text: Year-to-date combined ratio of 89.7 through H1 2018 despite $125 million in vehicle catastrophe losses and $82 million net Property catastrophe losses from 19 declared events in Q2.
YTD net premiums written growth of approximately 21% through Q2, with Commercial Lines TNC premiums up nearly 120% quarter over quarter after Uber expansion to four states.
Progressive moved from #4 to #3 in the private passenger auto marketplace, a milestone the letter calls one big step closer to the ultimate goal of becoming consumers' number one choice.

Photo estimating used for over 6.3 million customer-submitted photos in 18 months; nearly 75% of eligible customers accept the process; estimates returned an average two days faster than traditional inspection.

Machine learning AI algorithm to flag real-time estimate accuracy opportunities planned for August; texting solution used over 3 million times by claims reps since February rollout; eSign pilot completed 11,000+ forms.
HQX buy button launched in Wisconsin allowing online home purchase; rolled out to all available states for bundled auto and home quoting, providing lift to Robinson sales in both channels.

19 catastrophes declared in Q2 including Colorado hailstorms, Texas flooding, and Hawaii Kilauea eruption; companywide catastrophe losses still down 12% versus Q2 2017; new Nashville CAT facility leased.
Investment portfolio returned 0.6% for Q2 with equities up 3.1%; corporate bond portfolio expanded as valuations improved; five-year compounded equity return 25% annually, average ROE 18%.
Progressive formed an eleven-person Strategy Council in December 2017 and appointed Andrew Quigg as Chief Strategy Officer effective July 1, 2018, to lead dedicated Horizon Three exploration efforts.
